food_name: Maggi Noodle with Squid (Mi Goi Xao Muc)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Maggi instant noodle stir-fry with squid. FOODON_00002187 (noodle dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta and noodles, cooked) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for instant noodle stir-fry with squid in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific prepared noodle dish with squid.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Maggi noodle with squid is approximately 350 g, typically presented as a single plate at a hawker stall or casual restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Fried Noodles with Seafood (Mee Goreng Seafood) — adapted with squid composition data from USDA FoodData Central squid entry and HPB Singapore nutrient reference for stir-fried instant noodles
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB entry for Maggi noodle stir-fry with squid. Values estimated by adapting HPB Singapore data for stir-fried instant noodles (mee goreng) and incorporating USDA FoodData Central squid (cooked) composition data (NDB 15149). Weighted blend reflects approximately 55% noodle base and 45% squid and sauce components. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Instant noodles, boiled — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; adapted for stir-fried preparation with squid
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 52–54 for boiled instant noodles (wheat-based). A midpoint GI of 53 is applied here. Stir-frying with protein (squid) and fat further moderates postprandial glucose response; GI is not expected to increase materially above the boiled reference. GL per serving calculated as (53 × 61.25 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 32.5, rounded to 32.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1820 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load from instant noodle seasoning packets and soy-based sauces; a single serving may exceed 75% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (53) and high glycemic load per serving (~32)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in small portions; the wheat-based instant noodles produce a moderate glycemic response, but the large serving size results in a high glycemic load that may cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate cholesterol from squid
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content poses a cardiovascular risk through blood pressure elevation, while squid contributes dietary cholesterol (~145 mg per 100 g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and request reduced-sodium preparation where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Maggi Noodle with Squid (Mi Goi Xao Muc) is a popular stir-fried instant noodle dish served in Singapore's hawker centres and casual Vietnamese-influenced restaurants. The dish uses Maggi brand wheat-based instant noodles as its base, which are first briefly boiled and then wok-tossed over high heat with fresh squid (sotong), egg, garlic, chilli, and a sauce blend of soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil. The result is a savoury, slightly smoky dish with tender squid, springy noodles, and a glossy umami-rich coating. The Vietnamese name 'Mi Goi Xao Muc' reflects the dish's Vietnamese culinary heritage adapted to the Singaporean hawker context, where instant noodles (mi goi) are stir-fried (xao) with squid (muc). Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein from squid and egg, moderate carbohydrate from the noodles, and is notably high in sodium due to the seasoning packet and sauces. The glycemic index is moderate (approximately 53), but the glycemic load per standard serving is high, making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The dish is not suitable for those on low-sodium diets and should be consumed in moderation by individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It contains gluten, shellfish, egg, and soy allergens.
common_user_queries: What is Mi Goi Xao Muc?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Maggi instant noodle stir-fry with squid. Nutrient values were estimated by adapting HPB stir-fried instant noodle data and USDA FoodData Central squid composition data. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled instant noodles. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB or USDA source data used for this adapted estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Trans fat is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central squid data and is consistent with the absence of hydrogenated fats in the dish as typically prepared. The dish is listed under Singapore cuisine but has Vietnamese culinary roots; the restaurant serving it (Thien Long Vietnam Restaurant) reflects this Vietnamese-Singaporean hawker fusion context. Halal status is marked false as squid preparation and sauce sourcing cannot be confirmed halal without restaurant-level certification. Price data preserved from input: SGD 13.90. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate based on typical hawker plate portions for noodle dishes in Singapore.
